segments.raw: **SEG** | [00:00-01:30] | standing | - | 0.2 | 0-0 | 0.2 | 1.0 | hand_fighting,distance_management,feeling_out | collar_tie_battle,stance,level_changes | Initial feeling out process. segments.strategy: Both are looking to establish dominant grips and test reactions without over-committing. Tackett uses shoulder posts to manage range against Galvao's forward pressure.

segments.execution: The initial phase is a neutral grip-fighting battle. No significant takedown attempts are made, and the athletes remain on their feet, resetting grips frequently.

segments.raw: **SEG** | [01:30-01:43] | scramble | Galvao | 0.6 | 0-0 | 0.8 | 1.0 | takedown_attempt,front_headlock,scramble | flying_takedown,front_headlock_control,whizzer_defense | Galvao attempts an outside leg hook takedown that transitions into a front headlock scramble. | Galvao aims to surprise Tackett with an unorthodox entry, forcing a scramble to capitalize on any opening. segments.notes: Galvao attempts an outside leg hook takedown that transitions into a front headlock scramble.
segments.strategy: Galvao aims to surprise Tackett with an unorthodox entry, forcing a scramble to capitalize on any opening. Tackett's goal is to stay upright and avoid getting entangled.
segments.setup: Galvao shoots an outside leg hook (kouchi gari style) and immediately snaps down into a front headlock as Tackett squares up.
segments.execution: Galvao's entry is slick, but Tackett defends well by keeping his hips back and securing a whizzer. Galvao attempts to roll through with double overhooks, but Tackett's base is strong, forcing a reset to standing.
segments.outcome: The takedown is unsuccessful, but Galvao shows his dynamic and creative wrestling ability. Tackett demonstrates solid fundamental defense.
segments.coaching: Galvao's willingness to attack from anywhere is a huge asset. Tackett's whizzer and base were key to neutralizing a dangerous, unorthodox attack.

segments.raw: **SEG** | [01:43-02:52] | guard | Tackett | 0.5 | 0-0 | 0.5 | 0.9 | guard_pass_attempt,guard_retention,De_La_Riva | open_guard_passing,leg_pummeling,De_La_Riva_hooks | Tackett is on top inside Galvao's active and flexible open guard, attempting to pass. | Tackett wants to apply pressure and pass, while Galvao uses his legs to create distance, off-balance, and prevent any pass from settling. | Following the scramble, Galvao sits to guard, and Tackett engages on top, looking to control the legs and initiate a pass. | Tackett attempts to control Galvao's legs, but Galvao is highly active, using De La Riva hooks and leg pummeling to disrupt Tackett's base. Tackett tries to stack, but Galvao's flexibility and frames in the armpits allow him to recover and maintain distance effectively. | Tackett is unable to establish a solid passing position. Galvao's guard retention is superb, neutralizing all of Tackett's passing attempts. | Galvao's guard is world-class. Tackett needs to be more aggressive in killing the hips or using explosive movements rather than just pressure to pass this style of guard.
segments.notes: Tackett is on top inside Galvao's active and flexible open guard, attempting to pass.
segments.strategy: Tackett wants to apply pressure and pass, while Galvao uses his legs to create distance, off-balance, and prevent any pass from settling.
segments.setup: Following the scramble, Galvao sits to guard, and Tackett engages on top, looking to control the legs and initiate a pass.
segments.execution: Tackett attempts to control Galvao's legs, but Galvao is highly active, using De La Riva hooks and leg pummeling to disrupt Tackett's base. Tackett tries to stack, but Galvao's flexibility and frames in the armpits allow him to recover and maintain distance effectively.
segments.outcome: Tackett is unable to establish a solid passing position. Galvao's guard retention is superb, neutralizing all of Tackett's passing attempts.
segments.coaching: Galvao's guard is world-class. Tackett needs to be more aggressive in killing the hips or using explosive movements rather than just pressure to pass this style of guard.

segments.raw: **SEG** | [02:52-03:12] | back_control | Galvao | 0.8 | 0-0 | 0.9 | 1.0 | inversion,back_take,body_triangle_attempt | guard_inversion,back_take_mechanics,body_lock | Galvao inverts from guard, takes the back in a scramble, and secures a powerful rear body lock. | Galvao uses the scramble initiated by Tackett's pass attempt to create an angle for a back take, looking to secure the most dominant position in grappling. | As Tackett pressures forward, Galvao inverts underneath him, using his legs to elevate Tackett's hips and spin to the back. | Galvao executes a flawless inversion, coming up behind Tackett. He immediately locks his hands around Tackett's waist in a rear body lock (seatbelt grip without the over-under). He uses this to flatten Tackett out and begin climbing for the back mount. | Galvao transitions from a defensive guard position to a dominant back attack, completely reversing the momentum of the match. | A beautiful display of modern jiu-jitsu. Tackett's forward pressure was used against him. To defend, he needed to control Galvao's hips earlier in the inversion.
segments.notes: Galvao inverts from guard, takes the back in a scramble, and secures a powerful rear body lock.
segments.strategy: Galvao uses the scramble initiated by Tackett's pass attempt to create an angle for a back take, looking to secure the most dominant position in grappling.
segments.setup: As Tackett pressures forward, Galvao inverts underneath him, using his legs to elevate Tackett's hips and spin to the back.
segments.execution: Galvao executes a flawless inversion, coming up behind Tackett. He immediately locks his hands around Tackett's waist in a rear body lock (seatbelt grip without the over-under). He uses this to flatten Tackett out and begin climbing for the back mount.
segments.outcome: Galvao transitions from a defensive guard position to a dominant back attack, completely reversing the momentum of the match.
segments.coaching: A beautiful display of modern jiu-jitsu. Tackett's forward pressure was used against him. To defend, he needed to control Galvao's hips earlier in the inversion.

segments.raw: **SEG** | [03:12-04:08] | side_control | Galvao | 0.9 | 0-0 | 0.6 | 1.0 | mount_transition,pressure_control,side_control_dominance | mount_entry,hip_pressure,crossface | Galvao transitions from the back to side control and then smoothly into a high mount. | Galvao recognizes the back take is being defended and transitions to another dominant position (mount) to continue his attack and solidify control. | From the rear body lock, as Tackett turns to defend, Galvao uses the momentum to slide his knee across the belly and establish side control, then mount. | Galvao uses a heavy crossface and hip pressure from side control to pin Tackett. He slides his knee over Tackett's stomach and steps his other leg over to secure a high mount, immediately controlling Tackett's posture and isolating his arms. | Galvao establishes complete dominance, moving from back control to side control to mount, putting Tackett in a very dangerous situation. | This is a masterclass in positional transitions. Galvao never settled; he constantly improved his position. Tackett needs to create frames earlier to prevent the mount.
segments.notes: Galvao transitions from the back to side control and then smoothly into a high mount.
segments.strategy: Galvao recognizes the back take is being defended and transitions to another dominant position (mount) to continue his attack and solidify control.
segments.setup: From the rear body lock, as Tackett turns to defend, Galvao uses the momentum to slide his knee across the belly and establish side control, then mount.
segments.execution: Galvao uses a heavy crossface and hip pressure from side control to pin Tackett. He slides his knee over Tackett's stomach and steps his other leg over to secure a high mount, immediately controlling Tackett's posture and isolating his arms.
segments.outcome: Galvao establishes complete dominance, moving from back control to side control to mount, putting Tackett in a very dangerous situation.
segments.coaching: This is a masterclass in positional transitions. Galvao never settled; he constantly improved his position. Tackett needs to create frames earlier to prevent the mount.

segments.raw: **SEG** | [04:08-06:05] | mount | Galvao | 0.9 | 0-0 | 0.5 | 1.0 | mount_control,submission_hunting,arm_isolation | mount_pressure,arm_triangle_setup,gift_wrap | Galvao maintains a heavy mount, applying immense pressure and hunting for submissions. | Galvao's strategy is to use heavy pressure from mount to exhaust Tackett and create openings for submissions like an arm triangle or armbar. | Galvao has secured a high mount and is using his weight to flatten Tackett out. | Galvao uses a crushing shoulder pressure (chin on face) and controls Tackett's head. Tackett's defensive posture is solid, but he's reactive. He needs to proactively create scrambles instead of just weathering the storm.
segments.notes: Galvao maintains a heavy mount, applying immense pressure and hunting for submissions.
segments.strategy: Galvao's strategy is to use heavy pressure from mount to exhaust Tackett and create openings for submissions like an arm triangle or armbar.

segments.execution: Galvao uses a crushing shoulder pressure (chin on face) and controls Tackett's head. He methodically works to isolate an arm, attempting to set up a gift wrap and looking for an arm triangle. Tackett is purely in survival mode, keeping his elbows tight and trying to bridge.
segments.outcome: Galvao maintains complete control for an extended period, draining Tackett's energy, though he doesn't secure a submission.
segments.coaching: Galvao's mount pressure is suffocating. Tackett's defensive posture is solid, but he's reactive. He needs to proactively create scrambles instead of just weathering the storm.

segments.raw: **SEG** | [06:05-06:10] | scramble | - | 0.4 | 0-0 | 0.9 | 0.9 | escape,reversal,scramble | mount_escape,granby_roll,scramble_dynamics | Tackett initiates an explosive scramble from the bottom of mount, successfully escaping and getting back up. | Knowing he's losing on control time, Tackett uses a burst of energy to create chaos and escape the bad position, willing to risk a scramble to get free. | Galvao shifts his weight to attack an arm, giving Tackett a small window of space to move his hips. | Tackett uses a powerful bridge and shrimp to create space, then executes a Granby roll-style inversion to get his hips out from under Galvao. segments.notes: Tackett initiates an explosive scramble from the bottom of mount, successfully escaping and getting back up.
segments.strategy: Knowing he's losing on control time, Tackett uses a burst of energy to create chaos and escape the bad position, willing to risk a scramble to get free.
segments.setup: Galvao shifts his weight to attack an arm, giving Tackett a small window of space to move his hips.
segments.execution: Tackett uses a powerful bridge and shrimp to create space, then executes a Granby roll-style inversion to get his hips out from under Galvao. The scramble is fast, and both athletes pop back up to their feet to reset.
